help.overview: The Planets menu turns KeepTrack into a solar system viewer: select any planet, dwarf planet, the Moon, or the Sun to center the camera on it. It is the quickest way to study cislunar and deep space geometry alongside the Earth orbiting catalog.
help.imgAlt: The Planets List menu with planets, dwarf planets, and other bodies
help.imgCaption: Click any body to center the camera on it.
help.howToUse:
1. Open the Planets menu from the bottom bar or press P.
  2. Click a body, for example Mars or Moon. The camera recenters on it and zoom limits adjust to the new scale.
  3. Press Home to return to Earth, or Shift+Home to recenter Earth in view.
help.tip1: Several moons (Io, Europa, Titan, and others) are listed but grayed out; they are planned for a future update.
help.tip2: You can also right-click empty space and choose a body from the context menu.
help.shortcutHome: Set Earth as the center body
